question_text: Which of the following distance-time graphs represents an object that is accelerating?

options.label: A
options.text: A straight line with a constant positive gradient throughout the entire journey

options.label: B
options.text: A curved line where the gradient becomes steeper as time increases

options.label: D
options.text: A curved line where the gradient becomes less steep as time increases

correct_answer: B) A curved line where the gradient becomes steeper as time increases
explanation: On a distance-time graph, the gradient represents speed. If an object is accelerating, its speed is increasing, so the gradient must become steeper over time, producing a curved line that curves upward. A constant gradient (option A) shows constant speed with no acceleration.
